lines.en_lit: that is needed for its own sake, Rav Shimi bar Ashi said: I found Abaye explaining to his son “And he shall slaughter slaughter by a non-priest is acceptable. Now, from where would you come From the fact that it is stated: “And you and your sons with you shall keep your priesthood” I derive even slaughtering.

the verse states: “And he shall slaughter the bull before the Lord, and the sons of Aaron…shall sacrifice the blood” from the collection and onward is a mitzva of priesthood. “And he shall place his hands and he shall slaughter taught the slaughter is acceptable by a non-priest.
Since, from the collection and onward is a mitzva of priesthood, why do I “The sons of Aaron shall put to the exclusion of flaying and cutting up
But still, is necessary it might have entered your mind to say since not a service that is indispensable atonement, not be required priests. teaches us priesthood is required.

Rather, from here: “And Aaron’s sons, the priests, shall lay out the pieces, the head and the fat” Since, from the collection and onward is a mitzva of priesthood, why do I “And shall lay out to exclude flaying and cutting up
but say to exclude the arrangement of the two logs, It is reasonable exclude a service similar to itself,
On the contrary, it excludes arrangement, which is similar

It cannot enter your mind as the Master said: “And the priest shall bring all of it near and burn it on the altar” carrying the limbs to the ramp. it is carrying the limbs to the ramp that requires priesthood, carrying wood does not require priesthood. This, the arrangement of the two logs, does require priesthood.
Why do I “and shall lay out to exclude flaying and cutting up But say that this too is for its own sake,
Rather, “And the priest shall burn all of it on the altar” for what come? to exclude flaying and cutting up

“The priest shall bring all of it near…the altar” this carrying the limbs to the ramp, carrying the limbs to the ramp requires priesthood, carrying wood does not require priesthood. Therefore, arrangement of the two logs does require priesthood, shall put for its own sake,
“And they shall lay out,” two “The sons of Aaron,” two; “the priests,” two We learn that lamb requires six

Rav Hamnuna said Rabbi Elazar posed a difficulty: This is written bull requires twenty-four he resolved “On the wood that is on the fire upon the altar” what is an item about which it is stated that wood and fire altar
